subject: UX & Research
title: The Power of Error Recovery
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Power of Error Recovery usable.

hook.text: Gmail introduced 'Undo Send' in 2009. It became one of the most beloved features in email history.

buildup.text: Humans make mistakes constantly. Products that let users recover from errors feel safe and trustworthy.

discovery.text: Ctrl+Z is the most used shortcut in computing. The ability to undo is a core expectation in every application.

twist.text: Destructive actions without undo create anxiety. Users hesitate, second-guess, and avoid experimenting.

climax.text: Build undo into every important action. Recovery builds confidence and encourages deeper exploration.

punchline.text: Let users undo mistakes. Safety drives exploration.

quiz.question: Why is 'undo' so important in product design?
options: It lets users recover from mistakes, building confidence and encouraging exploration
